lesson.title: Cointegration & VECM
lesson.body: Two I(1) series are cointegrated if a linear combination β₀ + β₁x_t + β₂y_t is I(0) (stationary). Cointegration implies a long-run equilibrium relationship. Spurious regression occurs when two independent I(1) series appear related (high R², low DW). The Vector Error Correction Model (VECM) models short-run dynamics while enforcing long-run cointegrating relationships.

rooms.question: Two I(1) series x_t and y_t are cointegrated if:
answers: There exists β such that y_t − βx_t is I(0)
Both series have the same trend
Their difference x_t − y_t is I(1)
rooms.explanation: Cointegration: the linear combination (spread) y_t − βx_t is stationary (I(0)), despite each series individually being non-stationary I(1).

rooms.question: Spurious regression occurs when two unrelated I(1) series are regressed. The symptom is:
answers: High R² and t-statistics but residuals are non-stationary
rooms.explanation: Spurious regression: two independent random walks regressed on each other yield high R² and significant t-stats, but this is a statistical artifact.

rooms.question: The Engle-Granger two-step test for cointegration involves:
rooms.latex: \text{Step 1: OLS of } y_t = \alpha + \beta x_t + u_t \text{. Step 2: ADF on } \hat{u}_t
steps: \text{Step 2: ADF test on } \hat{u}_t \text{ — reject unit root → cointegrated}
answers: OLS regression, then ADF test on residuals
ADF test on both series simultaneously
rooms.explanation: Engle-Granger: regress y on x, save residuals û_t. If û_t is stationary (ADF rejects unit root), the series are cointegrated.

rooms.question: Order the steps in building a VECM from cointegrated series:
items: Test each series for unit root (ADF)
Estimate cointegrating vector β (OLS in step 1)
Specify VECM with error correction term: Δy_t = α(y_{t-1} − βx_{t-1}) + ...
rooms.explanation: Unit root tests first, cointegration tests, estimate long-run relationship, then build VECM incorporating the error correction mechanism.

rooms.question: In the VECM error correction term α(y_{t-1} − βx_{t-1}), the parameter α represents:
answers: Speed of adjustment back to long-run equilibrium
rooms.explanation: α < 0 means the series corrects when above equilibrium. |α| is the speed: large |α| → fast mean reversion; small |α| → slow adjustment.

boss.name: The Spurious Oracle
boss.taunt: Your regressions are significant. But are they real? I deceive with R-squared.

questions.question: The Johansen test, unlike Engle-Granger, can test for:
answers: Multiple cointegrating vectors in a multivariate system
Cointegration of exactly two series only
questions.explanation: Johansen allows testing cointegrating rank r (number of cointegrating vectors) in a system of n I(1) variables. Engle-Granger is limited to one cointegrating vector.

questions.question: If two I(1) series are cointegrated, what is the correct modeling approach?
questions.explanation: Cointegrated series should be modeled with VECM, which respects both short-run dynamics and the long-run cointegrating relationship. VAR in differences loses the long-run information.

questions.question: In pairs trading, cointegration is preferred over correlation because:
answers: Cointegration implies the spread is stationary (mean-reverting), enabling reliable entry/exit
questions.explanation: High correlation can be spurious or temporary. Cointegration guarantees a stationary spread in the long run — a stable relationship to trade around.
